name: Erythrocercidae
description: Yellow Flycatchers are a small family of strikingly colored passerine birds endemic to sub-Saharan Africa. They are active insectivores that glean and hawk prey from foliage in forest and woodland habitats.

children.name: Erythrocercus
children.description: A genus of small, brightly colored flycatchers with rufous caps and yellow underparts.

children.name: Erythrocercus mccallii
children.description: The Chestnut-capped Flycatcher has a bright chestnut crown, olive upperparts, and yellow underparts. It inhabits lowland rainforest across west and central Africa.

children.name: Erythrocercus livingstonei
children.description: Livingstone's Flycatcher has grey upperparts, yellow underparts, and a rufous cap with a prominent crest. It is found in riverine forests and woodlands of eastern and southern Africa.

children.name: Erythrocercus holochlorus
children.description: The Little Yellow Flycatcher is a small, entirely yellow-olive bird with a pale eye-ring. It inhabits coastal forests and thickets in eastern Africa from Somalia to Mozambique.
